data.title: Core Scientific, Inc./tx SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Core Scientific, Inc., a leading provider of blockchain and high-performance computing (HPC) infrastructure, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report outlines the company's financial performance and strategic transition from bitcoin mining to colocation services, reflecting significant changes in revenue streams and operational focus.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $239.3 million, reflecting a decrease from $415.7 million in the same period of 2024, primarily due to a strategic transition from bitcoin mining to colocation services.
  • Gross Profit: $17.1 million, compared to $116.3 million in the prior year, indicating a significant reduction in profitability.
  • Operating Income: $(126.3) million, a decline from an operating income of $20.6 million in the previous year, driven by increased costs and strategic shifts.
  • Net Loss: $(502.8) million, an improvement from a net loss of $(1.05) billion in the prior year, reflecting efforts to manage costs and restructure operations.
  • Net Loss Per Share: $(1.48), compared to $(3.71) in the previous year, showing a reduction in loss per share despite ongoing challenges.

Business Highlights

  • Digital Asset Self-Mining Segment: The company generated revenue from digital asset self-mining, with a focus on mining bitcoin. The segment experienced a gross profit of $6.8 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $92.7 million in the same period in 2024, reflecting a significant decrease in profitability.
  • Digital Asset Hosted Mining Segment: This segment provides hosting services for third-party digital asset mining. It reported a gross profit of $4.8 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, down from $21.7 million in the same period in 2024, indicating a decline in segment performance.
  • Colocation Segment: The company has expanded into high-density colocation services, generating $5.5 million in gross profit for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, up from $1.9 million in the same period in 2024. This reflects the company's strategic shift towards colocation services for AI and HPC workloads.
  • Geographical Performance: All of the company's revenue was generated in the United States for the periods reported, with no significant geographical diversification.
  • Future Outlook: The company is transitioning from bitcoin mining to high-density colocation services, expecting to deliver 250MW of billable capacity by the end of 2025. This strategic shift aims to provide more stable and predictable revenue streams.
  • Operational Capacity: As of September 30, 2025, the company had a billable power load of approximately 895 megawatts and gross power of approximately 1,370 megawatts, indicating substantial operational capacity for its digital infrastructure.
  • Customer Concentration: A significant portion of the company's revenue is concentrated with one customer, accounting for 71% and 78% of total revenue for the three and nine months ended September 30, 2025, respectively, highlighting a reliance on a limited customer base.
  • New Production Launches: The company is constructing, refurbishing, reallocating, or converting most of its ten facilities to support AI-related workloads, primarily for its existing colocation customer, as part of its strategic transition.

SEC Filing: Core Scientific, Inc./tx [ CORZ ] - 10-Q - Oct. 24, 2025

data.title: AMPHENOL CORP /DE/ SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Amphenol Corporation, a leading provider of interconnect, antenna, and sensor solutions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth and robust operational performance, driven by strategic acquisitions and strong market demand across key segments.

Financial Highlights

Amphenol Corporation reported impressive financial results for the three months ended September 30, 2025:

  • Net sales: $6,194.4 million, a substantial increase from $4,038.8 million in the same period of 2024, driven by strong performance across key segments.
  • Gross profit: $2,359.3 million, up from $1,356.9 million in the same period of 2024, indicating improved cost management and operational efficiency.
  • Operating income: $1,702.3 million, compared to $819.5 million in the same period of 2024, showcasing effective expense control and increased sales volume.
  • Net income attributable to Amphenol Corporation: $1,245.7 million, a substantial rise from $604.4 million in the same period of 2024, reflecting strong overall financial performance.
  • Net income attributable to Amphenol Corporation per common share — Basic: $1.02, compared to $0.50 in the same period of 2024, highlighting enhanced shareholder value.
  • Net income attributable to Amphenol Corporation per common share — Diluted: $0.97, up from $0.48 in the same period of 2024, indicating improved earnings per share performance.

Business Highlights

Amphenol Corporation's operational performance for the third quarter of 2025 was marked by several key developments:

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported significant growth in its Communications Solutions segment, driven by the acquisition of the Andrew Business from CommScope. This acquisition has enhanced the company's product offerings in base station antennas and related interconnect solutions.
  • Geographical Performance: The company experienced strong performance in its international markets, particularly in Europe and Asia, which contributed to the overall growth in net sales. The foreign currency translation adjustments also positively impacted the company's financial results.
  • Sales Units: The company reported an increase in production volumes across its segments, with a notable rise in the Harsh Environment Solutions segment due to increased demand in the defense and industrial markets.
  • New Product Launches: The acquisition of the Andrew Business has allowed the company to introduce advanced antenna and interconnect products, which are expected to drive future growth in the wireless networks market.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued growth in its Communications Solutions segment, supported by the integration of the Andrew Business and the expected acquisition of CommScope's Connectivity and Cable Solutions Business. This acquisition is projected to close by the end of the first quarter of 2026 and is expected to further expand the company's capabilities in the IT datacom and communications networks markets.

SEC Filing: AMPHENOL CORP /DE/ [ APH ] - 10-Q - Oct. 24, 2025

data.title: General Dyamics Stock Rises on Earnings. Aerospace Was a Standout. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

Aerospace and defense businesses are humming right now. General Dynamics' stock rose after the company posted another strong quarter.

General Dynamics announced Friday third-quarter earnings per share of $3.88 from sales of $12.9 billion. Wall Street was looking for earnings of $3.70 from sales of $12.5 billion. A year ago, in the third quarter of 2024, General Dynamics reported profit of $3.35 a share from sales of $11.7 billion.

Aerospace revenue rose 30% with profit margins improving. Sales grew in marine and combat systems by 14% and 2%, respectively, with profit margins stable.

Orders in the quarter totaled $19.3 billion. The book-to-bill ratio, defined as orders divided by revenue, was 1.5 to 1. Investors like to see a book-to-bill greater than one.

General Dynamics stock rose 2.7% to $350.77 on Friday, while the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average gained 0.8% and 1%, respectively.

"Each of our [business] segments grew earnings and backlog in the quarter, reflecting solid execution coupled with growing demand," said CEO Phebe Novakovic in a news release. "The aerospace segment in particular performed impressively ... with order activity for business jets remaining very strong."

General Dynamics Gulfstream division delivered its first G800 business jet in August.

Results looked solid in what has been a good year for shares. General Dynamics has topped Wall Street estimates all three quarters this year by an average of roughly 5%.

Coming into Friday, General Dynamics has risen about 30% this year, boosted by rising defense spending around the globe and growing demand for business jets.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Mergers and acquisitions
data.description:
The following bids, mergers, acquisitions and disposals were reported by 2000 GMT on Friday:

** Two Grindr GRND.N board members, part of an investor group that owns more than 60% of the company, have proposed to take one of the world's most recognized dating platforms for the LGBTQIA+ community private for about $3.46 billion.

** Public utility company Santee Cooper said on Friday it has approved a letter of intent from Brookfield Asset Management BAM.TO to enter into talks over resuming construction of two AP1000 nuclear reactors in Fairfield County, South Carolina.

** Eli Lilly said on Friday it would acquire gene therapy developer Adverum Biotechnologies ADVM.O in a deal valued at up to $261.7 million, aiming to strengthen its pipeline with an experimental eye disease treatment.

** Standard Chartered <STAN.L> has agreed to sell its wealth and retail banking business in Uganda to South African lender Absa Group <ABGJ.J> for an undisclosed amount, the companies said.

** FG Nexus has agreed to sell a majority stake in FG Reinsurance and FG Re Solutions to Devondale Holdings for $3 million in cash, a $1.25 million note and 40% equity ownership of Devondale.

** The Swiss National Bank has sold its stake in Anglo-Australian miner Rio Tinto <RIO.AX>, <RIO.L>, adding to a series of recent divestments from companies in extractive industries, LSEG data shows.

** Japanese brewer Kirin <2503.T> has put its Kentucky bourbon brand Four Roses up for sale for $1 billion, the Financial Times reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

** Australia's GPT Group said that it is acquiring a 50% stake in Sydney's iconic Grosvenor Place office tower for A$860 million ($558.83 million), forming a new partnership with the Commonwealth Superannuation Corp.

** Nicolet Bankshares <NIC.N> is acquiring MidWestOne Financial Group <MOFG.O> for $864 million in stock, in one of the bigger bank deals of the year.

data.title: CBOT soy retreats from 1-month top on profit-taking; US-China talks eyed
data.description:
Chicago Board of Trade soybean futures ended lower on Friday on a round of farmer selling and profit-taking after the benchmark November contract (SX25) hit a one-month high, traders said, while market players awaited upcoming U.S. trade talks with China, the world's top soybean buyer.

  • CBOT November soybeans (SX25) settled down 3 cents at $10.41-3/4 per bushel.

  • However, for the week, the contract rose 22-1/4 cents or 2.2%, buoyed by hopes for a U.S.-China trade deal.

  • CBOT December soymeal (SMZ25) ended Friday up $1.80 at $294.10 per short ton while December soyoil (BOZ25) fell 0.60 cent to finish at 50.27 cents per pound.

  • Rallies this week triggered a round of soybean and corn sales by producers, analysts said, as the soybean harvest winds down.

  • U.S. President Donald Trump is set to meet next week with Chinese President Xi Jinping in South Korea, with Trump reiterating that soybean purchases will be a major point of discussion.

  • Top economic officials from the U.S. and China were due to arrive in Malaysia on Friday for talks to prevent a trade war escalation and keep next week's meeting between Trump and Xi on track. U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer will meet with Chinese Vice Premier He Lifeng to find a way forward.

data.title: KKR & Co. Inc. Announces Time Change for its Conference Call to Discuss Third Quarter 2025 Results
data.description:
NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--October 24, 2025--

KKR & Co. Inc. announced today that it has changed the time of its third quarter 2025 earnings conference call and webcast. The conference call will now be held on Friday, November 7, 2025 at 9:00 a.m. ET. The conference call was previously scheduled for 10 a.m. ET.

The conference call may be accessed by dialing (877) 407-0312 (U.S. callers) or +1 (201) 389-0899 (non-U.S. callers); a pass code is not required. Additionally, the conference call will be broadcast live over the Internet and may be accessed through the Investor Center section of KKR's website at https://ir.kkr.com/events-presentations/. A replay of the live broadcast will be available on KKR's website beginning approximately one hour after the broadcast.

ABOUT KKR

KKR is a leading global investment firm that offers alternative asset management as well as capital markets and insurance solutions. KKR aims to generate attractive investment returns by following a patient and disciplined investment approach, employing world-class people, and supporting growth in its portfolio companies and communities. KKR sponsors investment funds that invest in private equity, credit and real assets and has strategic partners that manage hedge funds. KKR's insurance subsidiaries offer retirement, life and reinsurance products under the management of Global Atlantic Financial Group. References to KKR's investments may include the activities of its sponsored funds and insurance subsidiaries. For additional information about KKR & Co. Inc. , please visit KKR's website at www.kkr.com. For additional information about Global Atlantic Financial Group, please visit Global Atlantic Financial Group's website at www.globalatlantic.com.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20251024969611/en/

Sygnum Bank bets on Bitcoin lending with multisign custody model
data.description:
Sygnum Bank has partnered with Bitcoin-backed lending platform Debifi to launch a multisignature lending product that allows borrowers to retain shared control of their collateral.

According to the Swiss digital asset bank’s announcement on Friday, the product introduces a Bitcoin-native multisign lending model that allows clients to retain control of their collateral through distributed key management, ensuring that assets cannot be rehypothecated.

Sygnum clients can take out fiat loans backed by Bitcoin in a setup that requires three of five key holders to authorize any transaction, allowing borrowers to track and verify their collateral directly onchain.

“While other banks require full custody for Bitcoin-backed loans, MultiSYG’s distributed key management means clients retain verifiable control of their collateral throughout the loan term – a growing demand from Bitcoin investors,” the bank said.

The product will debut in the first half of 2026 and will be available to all Sygnum Bank customers upon launch.

Bitcoin-backed loans are back

Bitcoin-backed loans are making a comeback this year. In April, Bitcoin mining company Riot Platforms used its stockpile as collateral to secure a $100 million credit facility from Coinbase Prime, Coinbase’s credit arm.

Coinbase Prime issued another $100M loan to mining company Cleanspark in September. The company secured a second line of credit of $100M backed by its Bitcoin treasury from Two Prime in the same month.

According to a Bloomberg report, Cantor Fitzgerald issued Bitcoin-backed loans to FalconX and Maple Finance in May. FalconX confirmed a facility exceeding $100 million under a broader credit arrangement, while Maple Finance completed the first tranche of its deal with Cantor.

data.title: Middlesex Water Raises Dividend by 5.9%
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Middlesex Water raised its quarterly dividend by about 5.9%, to 36 cents a share, from 34 cents.

The new payout, equal to $1.44 a year, represents an annual yield of about 2.4% based on Friday's closing price of $60.36.

The water and wastewater utilities company has now increased its dividend for 53 consecutive years, it said.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Plymouth Industrial REIT Announces Agreement To Be Acquired By Makarora For $2.1 Billion >PLYM
Gogo Operating Chief Departs
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Gogo Chief Operating Officer Michael Begler has stepped down.

The inflight internet provider on didn't say who would succeed Begler. His departure was not the result of any disagreement with the company or its board of directors, Gogo said.

Begler had worked at Gogo since 2013 and became chief operating officer on Jan. 1 of this year.

He will be entitled to severance payments, Gogo said.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Ford Stock Has Quintupled Tesla's Gains. What's Behind Its Meteoric Rise. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

Tesla is led by the world's richest human, Elon Musk, and is on the cusp of breakthroughs with robo-taxis and robots that bulls hope will transform the global economy. Ford Motor, however, is having a better year.

There are good reasons for that.

Friday afternoon, Ford stock was up about 37% this year, more than four times the 8% gain of Tesla. Three things are mainly responsible for the increase: tariffs, electric vehicles, and, perhaps most important, low expectations.

Ford found itself in a relatively solid position to start 2025, after President Donald Trump announced his sectoral tariffs on imported cars and car parts.

Ford manufactures about 80% of the cars it sells in America domestically, compared with the industry average of about 50%. That means tariffs hit Ford's earnings less than others, including General Motors'.

GM stock is up 29% this year — good, but still trailing Ford.

Next up, electric vehicles. Through this year's first three quarters, Ford's EV business lost $3.6 billion. Looking ahead, the company probably will have to sell fewer EVs because lower emission standards implemented by the Trump administration make it more advantageous to sell large, gasoline-powered vehicles, where Ford makes most of its money.

Ford CEO Jim Farley projects EV sales will account for about 5% of new car sales in America for the foreseeable future. That's about half the current levels. Getting beyond that will require more affordable EVs, he said on Ford's earnings conference call after Wednesday's close.

Finally, beating low expectations helps any stock. Investors and Wall Street started the year very worried about what tariffs would do to automotive earnings. For Ford, there were also issues of poor manufacturing quality and supplier disruptions that threatened its profits.

Things turned out to be better than feared. Ford will make about $8 billion in operating profit this year, excluding the impact of a fire at an aluminum supplier that is disrupting production of Ford trucks. Ford earned about $10 billion in 2024.

Everything adds up to outperformance. Tesla bulls would point out that this year's gains in Tesla stock created some $120 billion in stock market value, about eight times what the move in Ford generated.

Ford investors, however, don't care. They are happy with their big raise.

Ford investors, after all, tend to be value investors. Tesla investors care about growth potential. Ford stock trades for about 10 times estimated 2026 earnings. Tesla trades for closer to 190 times.

It all depends on what kind of investor you are.

Ford stock was up 11% in midday trading at $13.68, while the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average were up 1% and 1.1%, respectively. Tesla stock was down 2.6% at $437.34, leaving shares with a loss since it reported earnings on Wednesday evening.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: LAM RESEARCH CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
LAM Research Corp, a leading supplier of wafer fabrication equipment and services to the semiconductor industry, has released its Form 10-Q report for the quarter ended September 28, 2025. The report highlights the company's robust financial performance and significant operational achievements, driven by sustained investments in the Foundry market segment and strong demand in key geographical regions.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $5,324 million, reflecting a 3% increase from the previous quarter driven by sustained investments in the Foundry market segment and higher customer support-related revenue.
  • Gross Margin: $2,685 million, representing 50.4% of total revenue, an increase due to favorable changes in customer mix despite reduced factory efficiencies and higher material costs.
  • Operating Income: $1,829 million, indicating strong operational performance with a focus on cost management and efficiency.
  • Net Income: $1,569 million, showing a significant increase compared to the same period last year, supported by higher revenue and improved gross margin.
  • Net Income Per Share - Diluted: $1.24, reflecting the company's ability to maintain profitability and shareholder value.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company operates in two primary revenue segments: systems revenue and customer support-related revenue. Systems revenue for the quarter was $3,547,565,000, while customer support-related revenue was $1,776,608,000.
  • Geographical Performance: The company reported significant revenue contributions from China (43%), Taiwan (19%), and Korea (15%) for the quarter ended September 28, 2025. Notably, revenue from China increased compared to the previous quarter, reflecting strong demand in the region.
  • Sales Units: The company experienced an increase in systems revenue, driven by sustained investments in the Foundry market segment, indicating robust demand for its wafer fabrication equipment.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued growth driven by secular demand for semiconductors and technology inflections in the industry, such as 3D device scaling and advanced packaging chip integration. These trends are expected to increase the served available market for the company's products and services.

SEC Filing: LAM RESEARCH CORP [ LRCX ] - 10-Q - Oct. 24, 2025

data.title: KINDER MORGAN, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Kinder Morgan, Inc., a leading energy infrastructure company in North America, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2024. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational achievements, showcasing steady growth and strategic expansions in its core business segments.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: $4,146 million, reflecting a 12% increase compared to the same period in 2024, driven by higher commodity sales and service revenues.

Operating Income: $1,063 million, showing a slight increase from $1,015 million in the prior year, indicating stable operational efficiency.

Net Income: $654 million, marginally up from $651 million in the previous year, demonstrating consistent profitability.

Net Income Attributable to Kinder Morgan, Inc.: $628 million, compared to $625 million in the prior year, reflecting steady performance.

Basic and Diluted Earnings Per Share: $0.28, unchanged from the previous year, indicating stable earnings per share performance.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The Natural Gas Pipelines segment generated the highest revenue among the segments, with significant contributions from firm services and natural gas sales. The Products Pipelines and Terminals segments also showed strong performance, with notable revenues from fee-based services and leasing services, respectively.

Geographical Performance: The company operates extensively across North America, with a focus on transporting natural gas, refined petroleum products, and other commodities. The acquisition of a natural gas gathering and processing system in North Dakota is expected to enhance the company's presence in the Williston Basin area.

Sales Units: The Natural Gas Pipelines segment reported substantial volumes in natural gas sales, reflecting the company's robust infrastructure and market demand. The CO2 segment also contributed to sales through product sales and other sales categories.

New Production Launches: The acquisition of Outrigger Energy's assets, including a 0.27 Bcf/d processing facility and a 104-mile high-pressure rich gas gathering header pipeline, marks a significant expansion in the company's production capabilities in North Dakota.

Future Outlook: The company plans to declare dividends of $1.17 per share for 2025, representing a 2% increase from 2024. Additionally, Kinder Morgan expects to invest $3.0 billion in expansion projects, acquisitions, and joint ventures throughout 2025, indicating a strong commitment to growth and operational enhancement.

SEC Filing: KINDER MORGAN, INC. [ KMI ] - 10-Q - Oct. 24, 2025

data.title: HEALTHCARE SERVICES GROUP INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Healthcare Services Group Inc., a leading provider of housekeeping, laundry, linen, facility maintenance, and dietary services to the healthcare industry, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth and operational achievements, underscoring the company's robust performance in a competitive market.

Financial Highlights

Revenues: The company reported revenues of $464.3 million, reflecting a growth from $428.1 million in the same quarter last year. This increase was driven by heightened demand in both Housekeeping and Dietary services.

Operating Costs and Expenses: Operating costs and expenses totaled $418.5 million, with a notable rise in Selling, General, and Administrative expenses to $50.5 million from $46.9 million, indicating higher operational costs.

Net Income: Net income surged to $42.9 million, a significant increase from $14.0 million in the prior year quarter, highlighting improved profitability.

Basic Income Per Common Share: Basic income per common share was $0.59, compared to $0.19 in the previous year, demonstrating enhanced earnings per share.

Diluted Income Per Common Share: Diluted income per common share also rose to $0.59, up from $0.19, consistent with the basic income per share improvement.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company operates in two primary segments: Housekeeping and Dietary. Housekeeping services accounted for approximately 45.6% of the company's revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2025, and 44.8% for the nine months ended September 30, 2025. Dietary services accounted for approximately 54.4% of the company's revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2025, and 55.2% for the nine months ended September 30, 2025.

Operational Performance: Healthcare Services Group Inc. provides management, administrative, and operating expertise to housekeeping, laundry, linen, facility maintenance, and dietary service departments, primarily within the healthcare industry. This includes nursing homes, retirement complexes, rehabilitation centers, and hospitals across the United States.

Customer Contracts: The company typically enters into renewable one-year service agreements with customers, which can be canceled by either party upon 30 to 90 days' notice after an initial period of 60 to 120 days. The company manages both full-service and management-only agreements, with the former including day-to-day management and provision of supplies.

Future Outlook: The company is organized into two reportable segments, Housekeeping and Dietary, and continues to focus on managing customer departments, including cleaning, disinfecting, sanitizing, and dietary services. The company is evaluating the impacts of the One Big Beautiful Bill Act on its income taxes, which may affect future operations.

Employee Retention Credit: During the three and nine months ended September 30, 2025, the company received significant Employee Retention Credit refunds from the IRS, which were recognized as a reduction in costs of services provided and interest income.

Goodwill and Intangible Assets: The company reported an increase in goodwill due to acquisitions, with a total of $80.1 million as of September 30, 2025. This reflects the company's strategic acquisitions to enhance its operational capabilities.

Lease Operations: The company recognizes right-of-use assets and lease liabilities for various operational needs, including automobiles, office buildings, and IT equipment, with remaining lease terms ranging from less than 1 year to 5 years.

Deferred Compensation Plan: The company offers a Supplemental Executive Retirement Plan for executives and key employees, allowing for deferrals and company matches in the form of common stock, which impacts the company's operational expenses.

SEC Filing: HEALTHCARE SERVICES GROUP INC [ HCSG ] - 10-Q - Oct. 24, 2025

data.title: Baker Hughes Co SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Baker Hughes Co, a leading energy technology company, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial and operational performance, highlighting key metrics and future outlooks in the context of the energy sector's evolving landscape.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $7,010 million, increased by 1% compared to the third quarter of 2024, driven by a 15% increase in Industrial & Energy Technology (IET) revenue.
  • Net Income: $617 million, a decrease of 20% compared to the third quarter of 2024, impacted by a decline in the market-to-market adjustment of certain equity securities and transaction costs.
  • Net Income Attributable to Baker Hughes Company: $609 million, reflecting a decrease from the previous year due to lower volume and increased transaction costs.
  • Basic Income per Class A Common Stock: $0.62, reflecting a decrease from $0.77 in the third quarter of 2024.
  • Diluted Income per Class A Common Stock: $0.61, reflecting a decrease from $0.77 in the third quarter of 2024.
  • Commentary: The company experienced a decline in net income due to lower cost productivity and increased transaction costs, despite efforts to improve efficiencies and modernize operations.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The Oilfield Services & Equipment (OFSE) segment reported a decrease in revenue, driven by declines in Well Construction, Completions, Intervention, and Measurements, Production Solutions, and Subsea & Surface Pressure Systems. The Industrial & Energy Technology (IET) segment saw an increase, with significant growth in Gas Technology Equipment and Gas Technology Services.
  • Geographical Performance: OFSE experienced a decline in international revenue, particularly in Europe/CIS/Sub-Saharan Africa and Latin America, while North America saw a slight increase. The Middle East/Asia region showed resilience with a slight increase in revenue.
  • Future Outlook - OFSE: The company anticipates continued soft operator activity for the remainder of 2025, with global upstream spending expected to remain constrained until potential excess oil supply is absorbed.
  • Future Outlook - IET: The IET segment is expected to benefit from continued strength in LNG and gas infrastructure, with increasing opportunities in industrial and distributed power markets, particularly data centers.
  • Sustainability Initiatives: The company continues to focus on sustainability, with a commitment to reducing Scope 1 and 2 carbon dioxide equivalent emissions by 50% by 2030 and achieving net-zero emissions by 2050. Progress includes a 29.3% reduction in emissions compared to the 2019 base year.

SEC Filing: Baker Hughes Co [ BKR ] - 10-Q - Oct. 24, 2025
